articles.headline: India and Brazil Ink Landmark Agreement on Critical Minerals and Rare Earths Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India and Brazil have signed a comprehensive agreement to enhance cooperation in critical minerals and rare earth elements.
• The pact aims to secure supply chains for these vital resources, crucial for renewable energy, defense, and high-tech industries.
• Collaboration will involve joint exploration, processing technologies, and research & development, leveraging Brazil's rich reserves and India's growing demand.
• This strategic partnership reduces dependence on single sources and strengthens the 'Global South' collaboration in strategic sectors.
• The agreement underscores both nations' commitment to energy transition and technological self-reliance.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Brazil is a major global producer of several critical minerals.
Rare earth elements are vital for electric vehicles, wind turbines, and advanced electronics.
India's demand for critical minerals is projected to rise significantly with its green energy goals.
The agreement focuses on joint exploration, extraction, processing, and R&D.
Brazil is a member of BRICS, alongside India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the geopolitical implications of critical mineral supply chain vulnerabilities and how this agreement addresses them.
Analyze the role of South-South cooperation in securing strategic resources and fostering technological independence.
Evaluate the economic benefits for both India and Brazil, including job creation, technological transfer, and reduced import dependence.
Examine how this partnership aligns with India's broader foreign policy objectives and its vision for a multipolar world.
Critically assess the environmental and social challenges associated with critical mineral mining and how the agreement might address sustainability.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and Brazilian flags next to a handshake, with geological survey maps in the background.
articles.trivia: Brazil possesses the world's second-largest reserves of rare earth elements, after China.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Issues Guidelines on Data Fiduciary Accountability under New Data Protection Act
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has delivered a significant ruling, clarifying the accountability framework for data fiduciaries under the recently implemented Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
• The judgment emphasizes the 'duty of care' and 'reasonable security safeguards' to be adopted by entities handling personal data.
• It addresses ambiguities regarding consent withdrawal mechanisms and the responsibilities of fiduciaries in data breach scenarios.
• This ruling provides much-needed clarity for businesses and strengthens individual data rights, setting a precedent for future data governance.
• The court highlighted the importance of transparent data processing practices and robust grievance redressal mechanisms.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 replaced earlier IT Act provisions on data protection.
A 'data fiduciary' determines the purpose and means of processing personal data.
The Act introduces the concept of a 'consent manager'.
Penalties for non-compliance are significant under the new law.
The right to privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of the Supreme Court's guidelines on the operational compliance for tech companies and startups in India.
Discuss the balance between data innovation and individual privacy rights as interpreted by the judiciary.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 in safeguarding citizen's data and compare it with global standards like GDPR.
Examine the role of the judiciary in interpreting and strengthening legislative frameworks in rapidly evolving technological domains.
Suggest further reforms or institutional mechanisms required to ensure robust data governance in India.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade with a digital data flow overlay.
articles.trivia: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was passed after several years of public consultation and parliamentary debate.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Generation Navigational Satellite for Enhanced Accuracy
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a next-generation navigational satellite, part of the NavIC constellation.
• This satellite incorporates advanced atomic clocks and improved signal transmission capabilities, promising higher accuracy and reliability for positioning services.
• The mission aims to bolster India's indigenous satellite navigation system, crucial for both civilian and strategic applications.
• The enhanced NavIC system will improve services for disaster management, vehicle tracking, maritime navigation, and military operations.
• This launch marks a significant step towards self-reliance in space-based navigation technology.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: NavIC is India's independent regional satellite navigation system.
It was previously known as IRNSS.
NavIC provides two types of services: Standard Positioning Service (SPS) and Restricted Service (RS).
The constellation typically consists of 7 satellites (3 in geostationary, 4 in geosynchronous orbit).
Atomic clocks are crucial for accurate timekeeping in navigation satellites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of an indigenous satellite navigation system like NavIC for India's national security and economic development.
Analyze the technological advancements incorporated in the next-generation NavIC satellites and their implications for service accuracy and reliability.
Evaluate the potential applications of enhanced NavIC services across various sectors in India, including agriculture, transportation, and disaster management.
Compare NavIC with other global navigation satellite systems (GNSS) like GPS and GLONASS, highlighting its unique features and regional focus.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in expanding the global reach and commercial viability of NavIC.
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space, with the NavIC logo superimposed.
articles.trivia: NavIC covers India and a region extending up to 1,500 km around its boundary.

articles.headline: India's Q3 GDP Growth Surpasses Expectations Amidst Global Economic Slowdown
articles.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) for the third quarter of FY 2025-26 recorded a robust growth rate, exceeding market forecasts.
• The growth was primarily driven by strong performance in manufacturing, construction, and services sectors, coupled with resilient domestic demand.
• Despite global economic uncertainties and inflationary pressures, India continues to demonstrate strong macroeconomic fundamentals.
• Government capital expenditure and private investment revival are cited as key contributors to this sustained momentum.
• The data reinforces India's position as a fast-growing major economy, attracting further foreign investment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Investment models.
articles.source: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation
prelimsPoints: GDP is the total monetary or market value of all the finished goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific time period.
Q3 of FY 2025-26 refers to the period October-December 2025.
Manufacturing and services are typically the largest contributors to India's GDP.
Government capital expenditure is seen as a crucial driver for economic growth.
The National Statistical Office (NSO) under MoSPI releases GDP data.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's resilient economic growth in the face of global headwinds and assess the sustainability of this growth trajectory.
Discuss the role of government policies, particularly capital expenditure and Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes, in fostering economic dynamism.
Examine the challenges posed by potential global economic slowdowns and supply chain disruptions, and how India can mitigate their impact.
Evaluate the implications of this robust growth for employment generation, poverty reduction, and income inequality in India.
Suggest structural reforms and policy measures required to maintain long-term high growth rates and achieve India's economic aspirations.
articles.imageDescription: A upward trending graph with Indian currency notes and a growing factory silhouette.
articles.trivia: India's economy is projected to be the third largest in the world by 2030.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New PLI Scheme for Advanced Manufacturing and Green Technologies
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme aimed at boosting advanced manufacturing and green technologies in India.
• The scheme targets sectors such as advanced robotics, sustainable materials, carbon capture technologies, and green hydrogen components.
• It offers financial incentives based on incremental sales from products manufactured in India, encouraging domestic and foreign investment.
• The initiative seeks to enhance India's manufacturing capabilities, create high-skilled jobs, and integrate into global value chains for future technologies.
• This move aligns with India's 'Make in India' and net-zero emission commitments.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: PLI schemes provide incentives to companies for increasing domestic production.
The first PLI schemes were introduced in 2020.
Sectors covered include mobile manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, automobiles, and textiles.
Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources.
Carbon capture technologies aim to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of the new PLI scheme to transform India into a global manufacturing hub for advanced and green technologies.
Discuss the challenges in implementing such large-scale incentive schemes, including beneficiary selection, financial outlay, and market distortions.
Evaluate how this scheme contributes to India's climate action goals and its transition towards a green economy.
Examine the role of PLI schemes in attracting Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) and fostering technological innovation in India.
Critically assess the long-term economic implications of government intervention through PLI schemes on industrial competitiveness and market efficiency.
articles.imageDescription: Robotic arm assembling a futuristic green energy component, with 'Make in India' logo.
articles.trivia: India has launched PLI schemes across 14 key sectors to boost domestic manufacturing.

articles.headline: India Prepares for COP31, Reiterates Demand for Equitable Climate Finance and Technology Transfer
articles.explainer:
• As preparations for the 31st Conference of the Parties (COP31) to the UNFCCC gather pace, India reaffirms its stance on climate finance.
• India emphasized the need for developed nations to fulfill their commitments to provide adequate and accessible climate finance to developing countries.
• The nation also highlighted the critical importance of technology transfer for climate change mitigation and adaptation efforts.
• India advocates for a 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities' (CBDR-RC) approach in climate negotiations.
• Focus remains on scaling up renewable energy, promoting climate-resilient infrastructure, and enhancing adaptation measures.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
keyTerms: UNFCCC (United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change)
CBDR-RC (Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities)
prelimsPoints: COP31 will be held in late 2026.
The UNFCCC is an international environmental treaty addressing climate change.
Developed countries pledged $100 billion per year in climate finance by 2020.
CBDR-RC is a principle acknowledging different capacities and historical responsibilities of countries.
India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical and current position on climate finance and its implications for global climate negotiations.
Analyze the challenges faced by developing countries in accessing climate finance and acquiring climate-friendly technologies.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current international mechanisms for climate finance and technology transfer.
Examine how India's domestic climate actions, such as renewable energy expansion, strengthen its negotiating position on the global stage.
Suggest innovative approaches for mobilizing and deploying climate finance more effectively to achieve global climate goals.
articles.imageDescription: Globe with hands holding it, symbolizing global cooperation on climate change, with 'COP31' text.
articles.trivia: The first COP (COP1) was held in Berlin, Germany, in 1995.

articles.headline: Progress on Western Ghats Eco-Sensitive Zone Delimitation: MoEFCC Holds Consultations
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) held a series of consultations on the final delimitation of the Eco-Sensitive Zone (ESZ) in the Western Ghats.
• The discussions involved state governments, local bodies, and environmental experts to finalize the boundaries and regulations for the protected region.
• The aim is to balance conservation needs with the developmental aspirations of local communities, addressing long-standing concerns.
• The move follows recommendations from various expert committees (Gadgil, Kasturirangan) and Supreme Court directives.
• Delimitation will regulate activities like mining, quarrying, and polluting industries to preserve biodiversity.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
It spans six states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu.
The Gadgil Committee (Western Ghats Ecology Expert Panel) report was submitted in 2011.
The Kasturirangan Committee (High-Level Working Group) report was submitted in 2013.
ESZs are typically notified around Protected Areas (National Parks and Wildlife Sanctuaries).
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the rationale behind declaring it an Eco-Sensitive Zone.
Analyze the historical controversies and challenges in demarcating the Western Ghats ESZ, considering the recommendations of the Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees.
Evaluate the socio-economic implications of ESZ regulations on local communities and the importance of participatory approaches in conservation efforts.
Examine the role of federalism in environmental governance, particularly in resolving inter-state disputes and ensuring coordinated action.
Suggest a balanced approach that can reconcile conservation imperatives with the developmental aspirations of the region's inhabitants.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with a river and diverse flora.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountain range.

articles.headline: Quad Leaders Convene to Discuss Indo-Pacific Maritime Security and Supply Chain Resilience
articles.explainer:
• Leaders from the Quad nations (India, USA, Japan, Australia) met virtually to reinforce cooperation on Indo-Pacific maritime security.
• Key discussions focused on enhancing interoperability among their navies and sharing intelligence to counter illicit maritime activities.
• The summit also addressed strategies for building resilient supply chains, particularly in critical technologies and semiconductors.
• The leaders reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, upholding international law.
• This meeting underscores the Quad's evolving role beyond security to include economic and technological collaboration.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The Quad is an informal strategic forum comprising four nations: United States, Japan, Australia and India.
It aims to counter China's growing influence in the Indo-Pacific region.
Malabar naval exercise involves Quad member countries.
Supply chain resilience refers to the ability to withstand disruptions and recover quickly.
The concept of a 'free and open Indo-Pacific' is central to Quad's vision.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the Quad in maintaining stability and a rules-based order in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the evolving agenda of the Quad, from primarily security-focused to encompassing economic, technological, and climate cooperation.
Evaluate the challenges and criticisms faced by the Quad as a grouping, particularly from nations outside the bloc.
Examine India's role and interests within the Quad framework, balancing its strategic autonomy with multilateral cooperation.
Suggest ways in which the Quad can enhance its effectiveness in addressing emerging geopolitical and geoeconomic challenges.
articles.imageDescription: Four national flags (India, USA, Japan, Australia) arranged around a map of the Indo-Pacific.
articles.trivia: The idea of the Quad was first mooted by Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe in 2007.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress and Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual review of the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlighted significant progress in curriculum reform, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges persist in teacher training, infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access across all regions.
• The review emphasized the importance of stakeholder collaboration for successful NEP implementation at state and district levels.
• Recommendations include accelerated capacity building for educators and enhanced funding for foundational literacy and numeracy programs.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Vocational education is to be integrated from Class 6 onwards.
The policy aims for 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in preschool to secondary level by 2030.
National Research Foundation (NRF) is a key recommendation of NEP 2020.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in addressing the shortcomings of India's education system and preparing students for the 21st century workforce.
Discuss the major achievements and persistent challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020 across different levels of education (school, higher, vocational).
Evaluate the role of technology and digital initiatives in bridging educational disparities and enhancing learning outcomes under NEP.
Examine the importance of teacher capacity building and curriculum reform for the successful realization of NEP's goals.
Suggest concrete policy measures and collaborative strategies to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to quality education for all.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom, with digital tablets and a teacher engaging them.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third education policy in India's history.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Significant Artifacts at Harappan Site in Haryana
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a new set of significant artifacts at a previously unexplored Harappan site in Haryana.
• The findings include well-preserved pottery, seals, tools, and evidence of advanced urban planning, providing fresh insights into the mature Harappan period.
• This excavation sheds new light on the geographical spread and cultural practices of the Indus Valley Civilization in the Ghaggar-Hakra river basin.
• The site's strategic location suggests its role as a potential trade hub or a specialized craft center.
• Further research is expected to redefine aspects of Harappan social structure and economic activities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: The Indus Valley Civilization is one of the three early civilizations of the Near East and South Asia.
Major Harappan sites in India include Rakhigarhi, Dholavira, Lothal, and Kalibangan.
Rakhigarhi in Haryana is one of the largest Harappan sites.
The Ghaggar-Hakra river system is believed to be the ancient Saraswati River.
ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in challenging or reinforcing existing theories about the Indus Valley Civilization's spread and characteristics.
Analyze how new findings, such as those in Haryana, contribute to our understanding of Harappan urban planning, economic activities, and social hierarchy.
Evaluate the methods and challenges involved in archaeological excavations and the role of interdisciplinary research in interpreting ancient civilizations.
Examine the importance of preserving archaeological sites and artifacts for understanding India's ancient past and cultural heritage.
Discuss the potential of such discoveries to attract heritage tourism and boost local economies, while ensuring sustainable practices.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating an ancient site, revealing pottery fragments.
articles.trivia: The Indus Valley Civilization is also known as the Harappan Civilization because Harappa was the first site to be excavated.

articles.headline: India Champions Digital Public Infrastructure at G20 Finance Ministers Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting, emphasizing the role of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• India showcased its successful DPI model, including Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, as a blueprint for inclusive digital transformation globally.
• Discussions focused on leveraging DPI for financial inclusion, efficient public service delivery, and fostering economic growth in developing nations.
• The meeting highlighted the need for global cooperation in building interoperable digital platforms and addressing digital divides.
• India's presidency agenda continues to push for a common framework and investment in DPI for sustainable development.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
India held the G20 Presidency in 2023.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by UIDAI.
UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
DigiLocker is a cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents.
mainsPoints: Discuss how India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model has revolutionized financial inclusion and public service delivery within the country.
Analyze the potential of exporting India's DPI model to other developing nations and the challenges associated with its global adoption.
Evaluate the role of global forums like G20 in fostering international cooperation for building resilient and inclusive digital ecosystems.
Examine the policy implications for India in championing DPI, balancing innovation with data privacy and security concerns.
Suggest strategies for leveraging DPI to achieve sustainable development goals and bridge the digital divide globally.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo with digital network lines connecting various countries, highlighting India.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 100 billion transactions in 2023.

articles.headline: National Urban Flood Resilience Plan Launched to Mitigate City Flooding Risks
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the 'National Urban Flood Resilience Plan' to address increasing flood risks in Indian cities.
• The plan integrates early warning systems, improved drainage infrastructure, and nature-based solutions like urban wetlands and green spaces.
• It emphasizes inter-agency coordination, community engagement, and capacity building for municipal bodies.
• The initiative comes in the wake of severe urban flooding events experienced across several metropolitan areas in recent years.
• The plan aims to build climate-resilient urban centers and protect lives and livelihoods from extreme weather events.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Urban flooding is distinct from rural flooding due to rapid runoff and inadequate drainage.
Factors contributing to urban flooding include unplanned urbanization, encroachment of wetlands, and climate change.
The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) provides guidelines for urban flood management.
Green infrastructure includes parks, urban forests, and permeable surfaces.
Early warning systems are critical for timely evacuation and preparedness.
mainsPoints: Analyze the primary causes and increasing frequency of urban flooding in India and its socio-economic impacts.
Discuss the key components of the National Urban Flood Resilience Plan and evaluate its potential effectiveness in mitigating flood risks.
Examine the challenges in implementing comprehensive urban flood management strategies, including coordination issues, funding, and public participation.
Evaluate the role of nature-based solutions and climate-resilient infrastructure in making Indian cities more adaptable to extreme weather events.
Suggest policy recommendations for strengthening urban governance and planning to build truly flood-resilient cities in India.
articles.imageDescription: A city street during heavy rainfall, with water levels rising, showing resilient infrastructure.
articles.trivia: Mumbai, Chennai, and Bengaluru are among Indian cities highly vulnerable to urban flooding.

articles.headline: India's First Privately Developed Jet Engine Successfully Undergoes Ground Testing
articles.explainer:
• A leading Indian private aerospace firm announced the successful ground testing of India's first indigenously developed private sector jet engine.
• This achievement marks a significant milestone in India's journey towards self-reliance in advanced defense and aerospace manufacturing.
• The engine is designed for potential applications in Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) and light combat aircraft.
• The development highlights the growing capabilities of India's private sector in cutting-edge defense technology and R&D.
• This initiative is expected to boost domestic innovation and reduce reliance on foreign imports for critical aerospace components.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: DRDO is the primary government agency for defense R&D in India.
The 'Make in India' initiative encourages domestic manufacturing.
Private sector participation in defense production is a key government policy.
Jet engines operate on the principle of Newton's third law of motion.
UAVs are increasingly used for surveillance, reconnaissance, and combat.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of private sector involvement in India's defense manufacturing and its contribution to 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defense.
Analyze the technological challenges and breakthroughs involved in developing an indigenous jet engine and its potential applications in military and civilian aviation.
Evaluate the impact of this development on India's defense imports and its strategic autonomy in aerospace technology.
Examine the policy support and ecosystem required to further foster private R&D and manufacturing in critical defense technologies.
Suggest a roadmap for India to become a global leader in aerospace manufacturing, leveraging both public and private sector capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: A sleek jet engine on a test stand, with engineers observing data on screens.
articles.trivia: Only a handful of countries possess the technology to design and manufacture advanced jet engines.

articles.headline: New Policy Framework for Sustainable Marine Aquaculture to Boost Blue Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying unveiled a new comprehensive policy framework for sustainable marine aquaculture.
• The policy aims to promote responsible farming practices, enhance seafood production, and ensure environmental sustainability in coastal areas.
• It introduces measures for disease management, genetic improvement of species, and better market access for aquaculturists.
• The initiative is expected to significantly contribute to India's 'Blue Economy' objectives, creating jobs and increasing exports.
• Focus is also on supporting small and marginal farmers through technological interventions and financial assistance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.

Agriculture: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
articles.source: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying
prelimsPoints: The Blue Economy refers to sustainable use of ocean resources for economic growth.
India is one of the largest fish producing countries in the world.
PMMSY (Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana) is a flagship scheme for fisheries sector.
Aquaculture involves cultivating aquatic organisms.
Mariculture is a specialized branch of aquaculture involving marine organisms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of sustainable marine aquaculture to boost India's Blue Economy and contribute to food security and rural livelihoods.
Analyze the environmental challenges associated with marine aquaculture and how the new policy aims to mitigate them.
Evaluate the economic benefits of promoting marine aquaculture, including job creation, export potential, and diversification of farmers' incomes.
Examine the role of technological interventions and government support in developing a robust and sustainable marine aquaculture sector in India.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable distribution of benefits and address the concerns of traditional fishing communities in the context of expanding aquaculture.
articles.imageDescription: Fish farms in coastal waters, with boats and fishermen.

articles.headline: India Considers Regulatory Framework for Emerging Space Tourism Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Space is reportedly drafting a regulatory framework to govern the nascent space tourism sector in India.
• The move aims to facilitate private sector participation, ensure safety standards, and manage liability aspects for sub-orbital and orbital space flights.
• This initiative aligns with India's broader space reforms, encouraging commercialization and private investment in space activities.
• The policy is expected to cover aspects like licensing, passenger eligibility, environmental impact, and international collaboration.
• India seeks to tap into the growing global space tourism market while prioritizing safety and sustainability.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Space tourism involves paying customers traveling to space.
Sub-orbital flights reach space but do not orbit the Earth.
IN-SPACe is an autonomous nodal agency to promote, handhold, and authorize private sector space activities.
NSIL is ISRO's commercial arm.
Virgin Galactic and Blue Origin are major players in global space tourism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential and challenges of developing a space tourism sector in India, considering technological, economic, and ethical dimensions.
Analyze the regulatory and legal complexities involved in governing space tourism, particularly concerning safety, liability, and environmental impact.
Evaluate how India's existing space infrastructure and private sector capabilities can be leveraged to foster a competitive space tourism industry.
Examine the socio-economic benefits and risks associated with space tourism, including job creation, technological spin-offs, and accessibility concerns.
Suggest a comprehensive policy framework that balances innovation, safety, and sustainability for India's emerging space tourism sector.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic spaceplane taking off from a launchpad, with tourists looking out of windows.
articles.trivia: Dennis Tito became the world's first space tourist in 2001, paying an estimated $20 million for a trip to the International Space Station.

articles.headline: India and Sri Lanka Enhance Maritime Security Cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka held high-level discussions to further strengthen their maritime security cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region.
• The focus was on joint patrolling, information sharing, and capacity building to combat illegal fishing, drug trafficking, and piracy.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to maintaining a secure and stable maritime environment, crucial for regional trade.
• The talks also explored possibilities for joint naval exercises and port calls to enhance interoperability.
• This collaboration is vital for addressing common challenges and ensuring freedom of navigation in critical sea lanes.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Sri Lanka is India's closest maritime neighbor.
The Palk Strait separates India and Sri Lanka.
Illegal, Unreported and Unregulated (IUU) fishing is a major concern.
India has SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) vision.
Colombo Security Conclave includes India, Sri Lanka, Maldives, and Mauritius as observers.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India-Sri Lanka maritime cooperation for regional stability and India's security interests in the Indian Ocean Region.
Discuss the common maritime challenges faced by India and Sri Lanka, including illegal fishing, drug trafficking, and ensuring freedom of navigation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing bilateral and multilateral mechanisms in enhancing maritime security and address the concerns of both nations.
Examine the geopolitical implications of external powers' increasing presence in the Indian Ocean and how India-Sri Lanka cooperation can counter potential threats.
Suggest policy recommendations for deepening maritime collaboration, including joint infrastructure development and disaster response mechanisms.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise in the ocean.
articles.trivia: India and Sri Lanka conduct the joint naval exercise 'SLINEX'.

articles.headline: Government Releases Policy Guidelines for AI Integration in Agriculture Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare released comprehensive policy guidelines for integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) into the agriculture sector.
• The policy aims to leverage AI for precision farming, crop yield prediction, pest and disease detection, and optimizing resource use.
• It promotes the development of AI-powered solutions to enhance farmers' income, improve efficiency, and address climate change impacts.
• The framework focuses on data governance, ethical AI use, capacity building for farmers, and fostering public-private partnerships.
• This move is expected to usher in a new era of smart agriculture in India, making farming more sustainable and profitable.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.

Agriculture: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
articles.source: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
prelimsPoints: Precision farming uses technology to optimize agricultural inputs.
AI can analyze satellite imagery, weather data, and soil conditions.
India's agriculture sector employs a significant portion of its workforce.
The National e-Governance Plan in Agriculture aims for digital transformation.
Ethical AI considerations include data privacy and algorithmic bias.

articles.headline: Proposed Heritage Protection Bill Seeks to Streamline Conservation and Management of Historic Sites
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture has proposed a new 'Heritage Protection Bill' aimed at modernizing the legal framework for conserving India's cultural heritage.
• The bill seeks to replace outdated colonial-era laws and streamline processes for identifying, protecting, and managing archaeological sites and monuments.
• Key provisions include enhanced penalties for vandalism, fostering community participation, and leveraging technology for site monitoring.
• It also addresses issues of illicit trafficking of artifacts and promotes sustainable tourism practices at heritage sites.

Discuss the key provisions of the new Heritage Protection Bill and its potential impact on the conservation and management of archaeological sites and monuments.

Examine the role of technology and community participation in safeguarding India's cultural heritage under the new legislative framework.

• Focus areas include increasing budgetary allocations for women's health, education, economic opportunities, and safety.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent India-Brazil agreement on Critical Minerals, consider the following statements:
1. Brazil is among the top global producers of several critical minerals.
2. Rare Earth Elements (REEs) are crucial for renewable energy technologies and defense applications.
3. The agreement primarily focuses on joint research and development, excluding exploration and processing.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Brazil is indeed a major global producer of several critical minerals. Statement 2 is correct: REEs are essential for various high-tech applications, including renewable energy and defense. Statement 3 is incorrect: The agreement explicitly aims for comprehensive cooperation, including joint exploration, processing technologies, and research & development, not excluding exploration and processing.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is/are correct?
1. It replaced the provisions related to data protection under the Information Technology Act, 2000.
2. A 'Data Fiduciary' is an individual whose personal data is being processed.
3. The Act introduces the concept of a 'Consent Manager' to facilitate data principals' consent.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, replaced earlier IT Act provisions on data protection. Statement 2 is incorrect: A 'Data Fiduciary' determines the purpose and means of processing personal data, while the 'Data Principal' is the individual whose data is being processed. Statement 3 is correct: The Act introduces the concept of a 'consent manager' to manage consent for data principals.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following applications:
1. Vehicle tracking
2. Maritime navigation
3. Disaster management
4. Military operations
Which of the above applications are served by India's indigenous NavIC satellite navigation system?
prelimsMocks.explanation: NavIC (Navigation with Indian Constellation) is India's independent regional satellite navigation system, which provides services for both civilian and strategic applications. These include vehicle tracking, maritime navigation, disaster management, and military operations, among others. Therefore, all the listed applications are served by NavIC.

prelimsMocks.question:
Regarding the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes in India, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. PLI schemes offer financial incentives based on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
2. The first PLI schemes were introduced in 2014 as part of the 'Make in India' initiative.
3. The new PLI scheme for advanced manufacturing targets sectors like green hydrogen components and carbon capture technologies.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: PLI schemes provide financial incentives based on incremental sales. Statement 2 is incorrect: The first PLI schemes were introduced in 2020, not 2014. Statement 3 is correct: The new PLI scheme specifically targets advanced manufacturing and green technologies, including green hydrogen components and carbon capture.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is/are considered a 'Biodiversity Hotspot' and spans across six Indian states?
(a) Eastern Himalayas
(b) Indo-Burma Region
(c) Western Ghats
(d) Sundaland
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity and spans six Indian states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu. The other options are also biodiversity hotspots but do not specifically fit the description of spanning six Indian states in the same manner as the Western Ghats.

mainsMocks.question: The India-Brazil agreement on critical minerals marks a significant step towards securing strategic resources. Analyze the geopolitical and economic implications of this partnership for both nations and the broader 'Global South'.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Significant Artifacts at Harappan Site in Haryana
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a new set of significant artifacts at a previously unexplored Harappan site in Haryana.
• The findings include well-preserved pottery, seals, tools, and evidence of advanced urban planning, providing fresh insights into the mature Harappan period.
• This excavation sheds new light on the geographical spread and cultural practices of the Indus Valley Civilization in the Ghaggar-Hakra river basin.
• The site's strategic location suggests its role as a potential trade hub or a specialized craft center.
• Further research is expected to redefine aspects of Harappan social structure and economic activities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: The Indus Valley Civilization is one of the three early civilizations of the Near East and South Asia.
Major Harappan sites in India include Rakhigarhi, Dholavira, Lothal, and Kalibangan.
Rakhigarhi in Haryana is one of the largest Harappan sites.
The Ghaggar-Hakra river system is believed to be the ancient Saraswati River.
ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in challenging or reinforcing existing theories about the Indus Valley Civilization's spread and characteristics.
Analyze how new findings, such as those in Haryana, contribute to our understanding of Harappan urban planning, economic activities, and social hierarchy.
Evaluate the methods and challenges involved in archaeological excavations and the role of interdisciplinary research in interpreting ancient civilizations.
Examine the importance of preserving archaeological sites and artifacts for understanding India's ancient past and cultural heritage.
Discuss the potential of such discoveries to attract heritage tourism and boost local economies, while ensuring sustainable practices.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating an ancient site, revealing pottery fragments.
GS1.trivia: The Indus Valley Civilization is also known as the Harappan Civilization because Harappa was the first site to be excavated.

GS2.headline: India and Brazil Ink Landmark Agreement on Critical Minerals and Rare Earths Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India and Brazil have signed a comprehensive agreement to enhance cooperation in critical minerals and rare earth elements.
• The pact aims to secure supply chains for these vital resources, crucial for renewable energy, defense, and high-tech industries.
• Collaboration will involve joint exploration, processing technologies, and research & development, leveraging Brazil's rich reserves and India's growing demand.
• This strategic partnership reduces dependence on single sources and strengthens the 'Global South' collaboration in strategic sectors.
• The agreement underscores both nations' commitment to energy transition and technological self-reliance.

India's demand for critical minerals is projected to rise significantly with its green energy goals.

Brazil is a member of BRICS, alongside India.

Analyze the role of South-South cooperation in securing strategic resources and fostering technological independence.
Evaluate the economic benefits for both India and Brazil, including job creation, technological transfer, and reduced import dependence.
Examine how this partnership aligns with India's broader foreign policy objectives and its vision for a multipolar world.
Critically assess the environmental and social challenges associated with critical mineral mining and how the agreement might address sustainability.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and Brazilian flags next to a handshake, with geological survey maps in the background.
GS2.trivia: Brazil possesses the world's second-largest reserves of rare earth elements, after China.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Issues Guidelines on Data Fiduciary Accountability under New Data Protection Act
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has delivered a significant ruling, clarifying the accountability framework for data fiduciaries under the recently implemented Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
• The judgment emphasizes the 'duty of care' and 'reasonable security safeguards' to be adopted by entities handling personal data.
• It addresses ambiguities regarding consent withdrawal mechanisms and the responsibilities of fiduciaries in data breach scenarios.
• This ruling provides much-needed clarity for businesses and strengthens individual data rights, setting a precedent for future data governance.
• The court highlighted the importance of transparent data processing practices and robust grievance redressal mechanisms.

prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 replaced earlier IT Act provisions on data protection.
A 'data fiduciary' determines the purpose and means of processing personal data.
The Act introduces the concept of a 'consent manager'.
Penalties for non-compliance are significant under the new law.
The right to privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of the Supreme Court's guidelines on the operational compliance for tech companies and startups in India.

Examine the role of the judiciary in interpreting and strengthening legislative frameworks in rapidly evolving technological domains.
Suggest further reforms or institutional mechanisms required to ensure robust data governance in India.

GS2.headline: Quad Leaders Convene to Discuss Indo-Pacific Maritime Security and Supply Chain Resilience
GS2.explainer:
• Leaders from the Quad nations (India, USA, Japan, Australia) met virtually to reinforce cooperation on Indo-Pacific maritime security.
• Key discussions focused on enhancing interoperability among their navies and sharing intelligence to counter illicit maritime activities.
• The summit also addressed strategies for building resilient supply chains, particularly in critical technologies and semiconductors.
• The leaders reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, upholding international law.
• This meeting underscores the Quad's evolving role beyond security to include economic and technological collaboration.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress and Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual review of the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlighted significant progress in curriculum reform, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges persist in teacher training, infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access across all regions.
• The review emphasized the importance of stakeholder collaboration for successful NEP implementation at state and district levels.
• Recommendations include accelerated capacity building for educators and enhanced funding for foundational literacy and numeracy programs.

GS2.headline: India Champions Digital Public Infrastructure at G20 Finance Ministers Meeting
GS2.explainer:

• India showcased its successful DPI model, including Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, as a blueprint for inclusive digital transformation globally.
• Discussions focused on leveraging DPI for financial inclusion, efficient public service delivery, and fostering economic growth in developing nations.
• The meeting highlighted the need for global cooperation in building interoperable digital platforms and addressing digital divides.
• India's presidency agenda continues to push for a common framework and investment in DPI for sustainable development.

• The talks also explored possibilities for joint naval exercises and port calls to enhance interoperability.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Next-Generation Navigational Satellite for Enhanced Accuracy
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a next-generation navigational satellite, part of the NavIC constellation.
• This satellite incorporates advanced atomic clocks and improved signal transmission capabilities, promising higher accuracy and reliability for positioning services.
• The mission aims to bolster India's indigenous satellite navigation system, crucial for both civilian and strategic applications.
• The enhanced NavIC system will improve services for disaster management, vehicle tracking, maritime navigation, and military operations.
• This launch marks a significant step towards self-reliance in space-based navigation technology.

GS3.headline: India's Q3 GDP Growth Surpasses Expectations Amidst Global Economic Slowdown
GS3.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) for the third quarter of FY 2025-26 recorded a robust growth rate, exceeding market forecasts.
• The growth was primarily driven by strong performance in manufacturing, construction, and services sectors, coupled with resilient domestic demand.
• Despite global economic uncertainties and inflationary pressures, India continues to demonstrate strong macroeconomic fundamentals.
• Government capital expenditure and private investment revival are cited as key contributors to this sustained momentum.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New PLI Scheme for Advanced Manufacturing and Green Technologies
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme aimed at boosting advanced manufacturing and green technologies in India.
• The scheme targets sectors such as advanced robotics, sustainable materials, carbon capture technologies, and green hydrogen components.
• It offers financial incentives based on incremental sales from products manufactured in India, encouraging domestic and foreign investment.
• The initiative seeks to enhance India's manufacturing capabilities, create high-skilled jobs, and integrate into global value chains for future technologies.
• This move aligns with India's 'Make in India' and net-zero emission commitments.

Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.

mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of the new PLI scheme to transform India into a global manufacturing hub for advanced and green technologies.
Discuss the challenges in implementing such large-scale incentive schemes, including beneficiary selection, financial outlay, and market distortions.
Evaluate how this scheme contributes to India's climate action goals and its transition towards a green economy.
Examine the role of PLI schemes in attracting Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) and fostering technological innovation in India.
Critically assess the long-term economic implications of government intervention through PLI schemes on industrial competitiveness and market efficiency.
GS3.imageDescription: Robotic arm assembling a futuristic green energy component, with 'Make in India' logo.
GS3.trivia: India has launched PLI schemes across 14 key sectors to boost domestic manufacturing.

GS3.headline: India Prepares for COP31, Reiterates Demand for Equitable Climate Finance and Technology Transfer
GS3.explainer:
• As preparations for the 31st Conference of the Parties (COP31) to the UNFCCC gather pace, India reaffirms its stance on climate finance.
• India emphasized the need for developed nations to fulfill their commitments to provide adequate and accessible climate finance to developing countries.
• The nation also highlighted the critical importance of technology transfer for climate change mitigation and adaptation efforts.
• India advocates for a 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities' (CBDR-RC) approach in climate negotiations.
• Focus remains on scaling up renewable energy, promoting climate-resilient infrastructure, and enhancing adaptation measures.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

CBDR-RC (Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities)
prelimsPoints: COP31 will be held in late 2026.
The UNFCCC is an international environmental treaty addressing climate change.
Developed countries pledged $100 billion per year in climate finance by 2020.
CBDR-RC is a principle acknowledging different capacities and historical responsibilities of countries.
India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical and current position on climate finance and its implications for global climate negotiations.
Analyze the challenges faced by developing countries in accessing climate finance and acquiring climate-friendly technologies.

Suggest innovative approaches for mobilizing and deploying climate finance more effectively to achieve global climate goals.

GS3.trivia: The first COP (COP1) was held in Berlin, Germany, in 1995.

GS3.headline: Progress on Western Ghats Eco-Sensitive Zone Delimitation: MoEFCC Holds Consultations
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) held a series of consultations on the final delimitation of the Eco-Sensitive Zone (ESZ) in the Western Ghats.
• The discussions involved state governments, local bodies, and environmental experts to finalize the boundaries and regulations for the protected region.
• The aim is to balance conservation needs with the developmental aspirations of local communities, addressing long-standing concerns.
• The move follows recommendations from various expert committees (Gadgil, Kasturirangan) and Supreme Court directives.
• Delimitation will regulate activities like mining, quarrying, and polluting industries to preserve biodiversity.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
It spans six states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu.
The Gadgil Committee (Western Ghats Ecology Expert Panel) report was submitted in 2011.
The Kasturirangan Committee (High-Level Working Group) report was submitted in 2013.
ESZs are typically notified around Protected Areas (National Parks and Wildlife Sanctuaries).
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the rationale behind declaring it an Eco-Sensitive Zone.
Analyze the historical controversies and challenges in demarcating the Western Ghats ESZ, considering the recommendations of the Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees.
Evaluate the socio-economic implications of ESZ regulations on local communities and the importance of participatory approaches in conservation efforts.

Suggest a balanced approach that can reconcile conservation imperatives with the developmental aspirations of the region's inhabitants.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with a river and diverse flora.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountain range.

GS3.headline: National Urban Flood Resilience Plan Launched to Mitigate City Flooding Risks
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the 'National Urban Flood Resilience Plan' to address increasing flood risks in Indian cities.
• The plan integrates early warning systems, improved drainage infrastructure, and nature-based solutions like urban wetlands and green spaces.
• It emphasizes inter-agency coordination, community engagement, and capacity building for municipal bodies.
• The initiative comes in the wake of severe urban flooding events experienced across several metropolitan areas in recent years.
• The plan aims to build climate-resilient urban centers and protect lives and livelihoods from extreme weather events.

Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

GS3.imageDescription: A city street during heavy rainfall, with water levels rising, showing resilient infrastructure.
GS3.trivia: Mumbai, Chennai, and Bengaluru are among Indian cities highly vulnerable to urban flooding.

GS3.headline: India's First Privately Developed Jet Engine Successfully Undergoes Ground Testing
GS3.explainer:
• A leading Indian private aerospace firm announced the successful ground testing of India's first indigenously developed private sector jet engine.
• This achievement marks a significant milestone in India's journey towards self-reliance in advanced defense and aerospace manufacturing.
• The engine is designed for potential applications in Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) and light combat aircraft.
• The development highlights the growing capabilities of India's private sector in cutting-edge defense technology and R&D.
• This initiative is expected to boost domestic innovation and reduce reliance on foreign imports for critical aerospace components.

GS3.headline: New Policy Framework for Sustainable Marine Aquaculture to Boost Blue Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying unveiled a new comprehensive policy framework for sustainable marine aquaculture.
• The policy aims to promote responsible farming practices, enhance seafood production, and ensure environmental sustainability in coastal areas.
• It introduces measures for disease management, genetic improvement of species, and better market access for aquaculturists.
• The initiative is expected to significantly contribute to India's 'Blue Economy' objectives, creating jobs and increasing exports.
• Focus is also on supporting small and marginal farmers through technological interventions and financial assistance.

GS3.headline: India Considers Regulatory Framework for Emerging Space Tourism Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Space is reportedly drafting a regulatory framework to govern the nascent space tourism sector in India.
• The move aims to facilitate private sector participation, ensure safety standards, and manage liability aspects for sub-orbital and orbital space flights.
• This initiative aligns with India's broader space reforms, encouraging commercialization and private investment in space activities.
• The policy is expected to cover aspects like licensing, passenger eligibility, environmental impact, and international collaboration.
• India seeks to tap into the growing global space tourism market while prioritizing safety and sustainability.

GS3.headline: Government Releases Policy Guidelines for AI Integration in Agriculture Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare released comprehensive policy guidelines for integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) into the agriculture sector.
• The policy aims to leverage AI for precision farming, crop yield prediction, pest and disease detection, and optimizing resource use.
• It promotes the development of AI-powered solutions to enhance farmers' income, improve efficiency, and address climate change impacts.
• The framework focuses on data governance, ethical AI use, capacity building for farmers, and fostering public-private partnerships.
• This move is expected to usher in a new era of smart agriculture in India, making farming more sustainable and profitable.
